content.problemStatement:
Pankhuri hates Algebra. Doesn't matter how hard she tries, she always fails to get good marks in her assessments. One of her marked assessments has been recently returned. She noticed that this time, the professor has only given per question marks and filled the total marks section with an expression consisting of +,- operations rather than a calculated number. For example - if there were 3 questions and she was awarded 10 marks in first question while 6 and 2 marks were deducted in second and third questions, then the professor would have written 10-6-2 in the total marks section.

Pankhuri might not be good at algebra but she is very smart. She came up with a brilliant idea. She plans to insert parentheses ((( or ))) at some places in the expression to create a valid bracket expression which evaluates to the maximum possible value. For example in the above scenario, she could create the expression 10-(6-2) which evaluates to 6 unlike the original expression which evaluates to 4. But the expression given to her is very long and she is not able to figure out the maximum marks that she can get.

Given an expression of N\text{N}N numbers and N-1 operators (either + or -) between them. We can enclose parts of the expression in parentheses to form any valid expression. What is the maximum value of the resulting expression?

###Input

First line contains a single integer T\text{T}T - the number of test cases.

For each testcase,

First line contains a single integer N\text{N}N. The second line contains the expression.

The input format is of the following form.

N\text{N}N

a1_11​ o1_11​ a2_22​ o2_22​ a3_33​ o3_33​ .. on−1_{n-1}n−1​ an_nn​

###Output
For each testcase, print a line containing a single integer representing the maximum value of the expression.

###Constraints
1≤T≤101 \leq \text{T} \leq 101≤T≤10

1≤N≤2001\leq \text{N} \leq 2001≤N≤200

1≤ai≤1091 \leq \mathbf{a_i} \leq 10^91≤ai​≤109

oi_ii​ is +++ or −-−

###Sample Input
3
3
8 - 1 - 25
2
38 + 20
4
40 - 8 - 1 + 25

###Sample Output
32
58
58

###Explanation
Test 1: 8 - ( 1 - 25 ) = 32

Test 2: 38 + 20 = 58

Test 3: 40 - ( 8 - ( 1 + 25 ) ) = 58
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains a single integer N, the number of numbers in the expression. The second line contains the expression of N numbers and N-1 operators (either + or -) between them.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a line containing a single integer representing the maximum value of the expression.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case, the maximum value is obtained by evaluating the expression as 8 - (1 - 25) = 32. For the second test case, the maximum value is obtained by evaluating the expression as 38 + 20 = 58. For the third test case, the maximum value is obtained by evaluating the expression as 40 - (8 - (1 + 25)) = 58.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to evaluate all possible expressions and find the maximum value. We can create a 2D array dp where dp[i][j] represents the maximum value of the expression from index i to j. We can then fill up this array by considering all possible expressions and taking the maximum value.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^3) — because we are using three nested loops to fill up the dp array
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — because we are using a 2D array of size N x N

code.Python3:
# COMPLETE, RUNNABLE Python3 code
import sys
MAX = 200
dp = [[-1 for _ in range(MAX)] for _ in range(MAX)]
nums = [0 for _ in range(MAX)]
ops = ['' for _ in range(MAX)]
def maxVal(i, j):
    if i == j: return nums[i]
    if dp[i][j] != -1: return dp[i][j]
    max_val = -10**9
    for k in range(i, j):
        left = maxVal(i, k)
        right = maxVal(k + 1, j)
        if ops[k] == '+': max_val = max(max_val, left + right)
        else: max_val = max(max_val, left - right)
    dp[i][j] = max_val
    return max_val
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(input())
    nums[:N] = list(map(int, input().split()))
    ops[:N-1] = list(input().split())
    for i in range(MAX):
        for j in range(MAX):
            dp[i][j] = -1
    print(maxVal(0, N - 1))
